differences.title: 1. MEMORY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Compile-time checks prevent memory errors without a GC.
differences.rightDesc: Automatic memory management simplifies development but can cause pauses.

differences.title: 2. CONCURRENCY
differences.leftDesc: Data race prevention at compile time through strict borrowing rules.
differences.rightDesc: Lightweight, concurrently executing functions managed by the Go runtime.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Zero-cost abstractions and fine-grained control yield exceptional performance.
differences.rightDesc: Efficient compilation and runtime offer excellent performance for many tasks.

differences.title: 4. SYNTAX & COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Advanced features like lifetimes and traits require deeper understanding.
differences.rightDesc: Minimalistic syntax and fewer features make it easier to learn.

differences.title: 5. ECOSYSTEM & COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Strong focus on systems programming, web assembly, and game dev.
differences.rightDesc: Widely adopted for backend, cloud services, and CLI tools.

differences.title: 6. ERROR HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Explicitly handles recoverable errors using `Result` types.
differences.rightDesc: Implicit error propagation via returned error values.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for operating systems, embedded systems, and high-performance web.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for microservices, distributed systems, and command-line tools.
